name: TAO 4h Post Expansion Partial Runner v1
edge_mechanism: after a directional expansion proves itself, partial profit is harvested quickly while a core runner is maintained for persistent trend continuation instead of full de-risking too early
thesis: Cycle 78 explicitly asks for a post-expansion partial-profit runner structure that keeps a core position for trend persistence. TAO 4h is the only lane in the basket where that management expression has a credible structural reason to exist. The research variable here is management structure itself, not just entry timing.
invalidation_condition: If TAO 4h post-expansion entries cannot support a runner after the first partial, or if the setup simply enters too late after the move, the thesis fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A directional expansion proves itself, early profits are harvested, and a remaining core position persists if the trend stays intact.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Some continuation entries are not wrong; they are simply over-de-risked. On a trend-capable asset like TAO, partial-profit plus held core can capture the minority of moves that actually persist.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h is the best fit because it is the only allowed lane with enough trend contamination to justify a held runner as a primary research variable.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: The entry may still arrive after too much of the move is spent, leaving the runner to absorb the reversal.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If the first partial works but the runner gives too much back, tighten the runner trail before changing the expansion proof.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Lower count than the inventory-reset idea, but with a management profile where a minority of runner wins matter materially.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: TAO 4h clears density and the runner contributes enough expectancy to justify further management-focused refinement.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-12 trades, or a pattern where the runner consistently gives back more than the first partial earns.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: acceptable because expansion proof is simpler than delayed retest confirmation chains

validation_basket.why_included: Best-fit trend-up lane for testing runner-oriented management after confirmed expansion.
validation_basket.continue_if: TAO 4h produces enough expansion entries and the held core adds meaningful expectancy.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: TAO 4h entries are consistently late and the runner becomes a liability.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Moderate count with a minority of strong runners carrying the result.

validation_basket.why_included: Useful control to see whether a pure chop lane suppresses the value of runner-based management.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH 4h still shows some persistence after expansion.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: ETH 4h confirms the management edge depends on trend context.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Sparse runner contributions with many first-partial exits.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same management expression has value in a weaker cross-lane, not just TAO.
validation_basket.continue_if: AXS 4h still allows partial-profit plus core persistence without giving everything back.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: AXS 4h proves the runner concept needs stronger trend contamination.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: More fragile runners and more partial-driven expectancy than TAO.

long: Trail remaining 50% by 0.94 * ATR_14 while close remains >= EMA_20
Time-based de-risk: cut 20% more after 7 bars if no 1.3 ATR follow-through
short: Trail remaining 50% by 0.94 * ATR_14 while close remains <= EMA_20
Time-based de-risk: cut 20% more after 7 bars if no 1.3 ATR follow-through
